chapter_title.ar: باب حَدِّ الْمَاءِ الَّذِي يُغَسَّلُ بِهِ الْمَيِّتُ وَ الْكَافُورِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 23 – A limit of the water to wash the deceased with, and (for) the camphor

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/323)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/323)
text: السُّنَّةُ فِي الْحَنُوطِ ثَلَاثَةَ عَشَرَ دِرْهَماً وَ ثُلُثٌ أَكْثَرُهُ وَ قَالَ إِنَّ جَبْرَئِيلَ ( عليه السلام ) نَزَلَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) بِحَنُوطٍ وَ كَانَ وَزْنُهُ أَرْبَعِينَ دِرْهَماً فَقَسَمَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) ثَلَاثَةَ أَجْزَاءٍ جُزْءٌ لَهُ وَ جُزْءٌ لِعَلِيٍّ وَ جُزْءٌ لِفَاطِمَةَ ( عليها السلام ) .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, raising it, said,

‘The Sunnah regarding the embalming is thirteen Dirhams and a third (in weight) at most’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Jibraeel<sup>as</sup> descended unto Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> with embalming (camphor), and its weight was of forty Dirhams. So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> divided it into three parts – a part for himself<sup>saww</sup>, and a part for Ali<sup>asws</sup>, and a part for (Syeda) Fatima<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father in a marfu‘ manner: “The Sunnah about hunut (application of camphor) is thirteen dirham (in weight) and one third at most. He (the Imam) has said that Jibril came to the Messenger of Allah with hunut (camphor) that weighed forty dirhams. The Messenger of Allah divided it in three part: one for himself, one for Ali, and one for Fatimah, ‘Alayhim al-Salam.’”
